channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: world
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: world

item.title: Trump threatens to bomb Iran unless they end nuclear weapons program and begin talks on new deal
item.description: Trump warned Iran after sending a letter to revive nuclear talks. With "maximum pressure" back on the table, tensions between the two nations continue to rise.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Smell of death permeates Myanmar cities after quake kills over 1,600 and leaves countless buried
item.description: MANDALAY, Myanmar (AP) — The smell of decaying bodies permeated the streets of Myanmar's second-largest city on Sunday as people worked frantically by hand to clear rubble in the hope of finding someone still alive, two days after a massive earthquake struck that killed more than 1,600 people and le
item.dc:creator: THEIN ZAW, DAVID RISING and GRANT PECK, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: What to know about Myanmar's civil war, which is complicating earthquake relief efforts
item.description: BANGKOK (AP) — The Southeast Asian nation of Myanmar has been plagued by wars and natural disasters since it became independent from Britain in 1948, when it was still called Burma.
item.dc:creator: GRANT PECK, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Chair of African charity Prince Harry co-founded says the royal orchestrated a bullying campaign
item.description: The chairperson of an African charity co-founded by Prince Harry has accused him of orchestrating a bullying and harassment campaign to try to force her out.
item.dc:creator: Brian Melley, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Landmark UK report on Hamas massacre exposes worst attack on Jews since Holocaust
item.description: A UK Parliament report chaired by respected historian Lord Andrew Roberts calls the Oct. 7 Hamas attack in Israel deliberate and brutal, urging democracies to combat denial and misinformation.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Officials survey damage in Myanmar's earthquake-devastated central areas
item.description: BANGKOK (AP) — Friday’s deadly earthquake rattled most of Myanmar and Thailand but certain areas sustained the heaviest damage, including flattened buildings and many lost lives. The death toll of the 7.7 magnitude earthquake on Friday rose quickly in Myanmar and was at 1,644 victims by Sunday.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: Pope's willingness to show his frailty provides an example to young and old alike in aging world
item.description: ROME (AP) — Pope Francis’ frailty was on full view as he left Rome’s Gemelli hospital last Sunday after five weeks battling pneumonia that nearly killed him. He could barely lift his arms to bless the crowd. His eyes were sunken, face bloated.
item.dc:creator: COLLEEN BARRY, Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: Medical supplies in great need as international assistance flows into Myanmar after earthquake
item.description: BANGKOK (AP) — Emergency aid has streamed into Southeast Asia in the two days since a massive earthquake struck Myanmar and Thailand. Relief efforts are focused on Myanmar, where the estimated death toll rose to 1,644 by Sunday afternoon.
item.dc:creator: BRIAN P. D. HANNON, Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: Hegseth announces plan for US command upgrades in Japan to deter China
item.description: U.S. Secretary of Defense Pete Hegseth announced a plan to upgrade the U.S. military command in Japan, an "indispensable" country against Chinese aggression.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Turkey arrests Swedish journalist over alleged terrorist connections to a pro-Kurdish group
item.description: ISTANBUL (AP) — Turkish authorities said Sunday they arrested a Swedish journalist dispatched to cover ongoing nationwide protests on charges of terrorism and insulting the president.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: 2 killed and dozens wounded in Russian attack on Kharkiv as Trump lashes out at Putin
item.description: Ukrainian officials say two people were killed when Russian drones struck a military hospital, shopping center and apartment buildings in Ukraine’s second-largest city of Kharkiv.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Myanmar quake death toll rises to 1,644 as resistance movement announces partial ceasefire
item.description: BANGKOK (AP) — A unilateral partial ceasefire to facilitate earthquake relief efforts was announced on Saturday by Myanmar’s shadow National Unity Government, which coordinates the popular struggle against the ruling military. The country's death toll from the disaster soared to 1,644.
item.dc:creator: GRANT PECK and JINTAMAS SAKSORNCHAI, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: With federal election looming, Liberal Canadian PM accused of plagiarism while studying at Oxford: report
item.description: Prime Minister Mark Carney is embroiled in controversy after a Canadian news outlet discovered at least 10 instances of possible plagiarism in his 1995 Oxford thesis.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Trump golfs with Finland's president amid icy relationship with EU
item.description: President Trump joined the leader of Finland, a member of the European Union (EU), for a round of golf Saturday, despite trade tensions with the executive body, which recently announced levies on agricultural products and clothing items imported from the United States slated to take effect in mid-Ap
item.dc:creator: Ashleigh Fields, The Hill

item.title: Syria swears in new transitional government 4 months after Assad's removal
item.description: DAMASCUS, Syria (AP) — Syria’s new transitional government was sworn in Saturday nearly four months after the Assad family was removed from power and as the new authorities in Damascus work to bring back stability to the war-torn country.
item.dc:creator: GHAITH ALSAYED and BASSEM MROUE, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Myanmar's earthquake death toll jumps to 1,644 as more bodies are recovered from the rubble
item.description: Myanmar’s ruling military confirmed death toll from a devastating 7.7 magnitude earthquake rose to 1,644, as more bodies were pulled from the rubble of the scores of buildings that collapsed.
item.dc:creator: David Rising, WFLA, NBC

item.title: New US strikes against Houthi rebels kill at least 1 in Yemen
item.description: DUBAI, United Arab Emirates (AP) — Suspected U.S. airstrikes pounded Yemen overnight into Saturday, reportedly killing at least one person as the American military acknowledged earlier bombing a major military site in the heart of Sanaa controlled by the Houthi rebels.
item.dc:creator: JON GAMBRELL, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Israeli settlers seen on camera assaulting a Palestinian village. Police arrest only Palestinians
item.description: JERUSALEM (AP) — Over a dozen Israeli settlers attacked a Palestinian village in the southern Israeli-occupied West Bank on Friday, beating residents with sticks and rocks, in an incident captured with rare clarity by security cameras.
item.dc:creator: JULIA FRANKEL, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Vance slams Denmark's handling of Greenland security
item.description: Vice President Vance on Friday slammed Denmark as failing to prioritize Greenland’s security, escalating rhetoric between Washington and Copenhagen amid President Trump’s claims to take over the island.
item.dc:creator: Laura Kelly, The Hill

item.title: UN chief: World leaders must speak with one voice to prevent a new civil war in South Sudan
item.description: UNITED NATIONS (AP) — The United Nations chief urged regional and international leaders Friday to prevent South Sudan from falling “over the abyss” into another civil war and speak with one voice to support a return to peace.
item.dc:creator: EDITH M. LEDERER, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Canadian company seeks US permission to start deep-sea mining as outcry ensues
item.description: SAN JUAN, Puerto Rico (AP) — An abrupt announcement rattled members of a little-known U.N. agency based in Jamaica that has protected international deep-sea waters for more than 30 years. The Metals Company in Vancouver, Canada said late Thursday that it is seeking permission from the U.S.
item.dc:creator: DÁNICA COTO, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: US, Japan, Philippines stage navy drills in disputed South China Sea as a Chinese ship keeps watch
item.description: ABOARD BRP JOSE RIZAL, South China Sea (AP) — The United States, Japan and the Philippines on Friday staged joint naval drills to boost crisis readiness off a disputed South China Sea shoal as a Chinese military ship kept watch from a distance.
item.dc:creator: AARON FAVILA, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Putin says Trump plans for Greenland 'has nothing to do' with Russia
item.description: Russian President Vladimir Putin says President Donald Trump's ambitions for Greenland has "nothing to do" with Russia as Vice President JD Vance lands to assess security base.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Powerful earthquake rocks Thailand and Myanmar, kills more than 150
item.description: A 7.7 magnitude earthquake rocked Thailand and neighboring Myanmar midday on Friday, burying an unknown number when a high-rise building under construction collapsed.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: Massive 7.7 magnitude earthquake rocks Thailand, Burma, collapsing buildings and killing more than 1,000
item.description: A massive 7.7 magnitude earthquake has caused buildings to collapse in Thailand and Burma, leaving more than 1,000 dead across both countries.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Knife-wielding man injures multiple people in Amsterdam, including 2 Americans
item.description: Dutch police are searching for a motive after five people, including two Americans, were stabbed in a busy shopping area of Amsterdam on Thursday afternoon.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: His brothers’ keeper: Liran Berman calls for urgent action to save his brothers, Gali and Ziv, from Hamas
item.description: Gali and Ziv Berman were taken hostage on Oct. 7, 2023, and their brother, Liran, has been fighting for their freedom ever since. He says his brothers were "the light of every room" they walked into.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Oscar-winning Palestinian director attacked by Israeli settlers, detained by army
item.description: Co-director Basel Adra witnessed the detention and said around two dozen settlers — some masked, some carrying guns, some in Israeli uniform — attacked the village.
item.dc:creator: JULIA FRANKEL, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: A Russian drone attack kills 7 in Ukraine ahead of ceasefire talks
item.description: KYIV, Ukraine (AP) — Russia launched a barrage of drones across Ukraine overnight Sunday that killed at least seven people, according to local Ukrainian officials and emergency services.
item.dc:creator: SAMYA KULLAB, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Pope Francis is back home after a 5-week hospital stay for life-threatening double pneumonia
item.description: VATICAN CITY (AP) — A frail Pope Francis returned to the Vatican on Sunday after a five-week hospitalization for life-threatening double pneumonia, and he made a surprise stop at his favorite basilica on the way home before beginning two months of prescribed rest and recovery.
item.dc:creator: NICOLE WINFIELD, PAOLO SANTALUCIA and TRISHA THOMAS, Associated Press, NewsNation
